title: Week of March 23, 2026
summary: This week brought major improvements to OpsPal's Windows setup experience with native installation support for all required tools. We also enhanced Salesforce assessments with better revenue field detection and sales process analysis, helping RevOps teams get more accurate insights from their audits. Behind the scenes, we strengthened platform security and resolved numerous stability issues.

entries.headline: Faster record lookups for single records
details: You can now quickly verify individual records without routing delays for simple lookups and small result sets

entries.headline: Native Windows setup installs all tools automatically
details: Windows users can now get OpsPal running with automatic installation of Git, Claude Code, and Node.js - no manual downloads needed

entries.headline: Improved Windows installation compatibility
details: Fixed PowerShell script issues that could cause installation failures on some Windows systems

entries.headline: Better revenue context tracking in Salesforce assessments
details: Assessment results now properly capture revenue field information for more comprehensive reporting

entries.headline: Customizable templates and brand gallery
details: You can now customize OpsPal's templates and branding elements while keeping them safe during platform updates

entries.headline: Smarter revenue field detection in Salesforce assessments
details: OpsPal now automatically finds your organization's revenue fields and analyzes sales processes for more accurate RevOps insights

entries.headline: One-click Windows setup with PowerShell
details: Windows users can now install OpsPal with a single PowerShell command for faster onboarding

entries.headline: Smoother setup experience and project detection
details: Fixed issues with project initialization and improved the overall setup flow

entries.headline: Prevent risky Salesforce deployments before they happen
details: New pre-deployment checks catch common configuration issues like restricted picklist values, duplicate settings, and field history limits

entries.headline: Automatic secret scanning prevents credential exposure
details: OpsPal now automatically scans your code for accidentally exposed credentials before commits and pushes

entries.headline: Enhanced secret detection and monitoring
details: Added comprehensive secret scanning configuration to catch more types of sensitive information

entries.headline: Removed hardcoded credentials from platform code
details: All embedded credentials have been removed and replaced with secure configuration methods
